_comment: firewall rules for web-app-sg. the main thing here is keeping ssh locked to my ip — 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22 is how most accounts get popped. http/https are open because it's a public-facing server.
Description: Firewall for web application EC2 instances

InboundRules.Description: SSH — my current IP only. if your IP changes you'll need to update this rule.
InboundRules.Rationale: opening port 22 to the world is probably the most common way aws accounts get compromised. even locking it to one IP is dramatically better than 0.0.0.0/0.
needed for normal web traffic. also if we add a load balancer later, it'll use port 80 for health checks by default.
once a cert is installed the server can serve https with no extra firewall changes. better to have this open from the start.

OutboundRules.Description: all outbound allowed — default aws behaviour. instances need this to reach s3, ssm endpoints, and yum/dnf package mirrors.
